steps.title: Identify the slope of line k
steps.content: Line k: $y = 7x + 2$. This is in slope-intercept form, so slope $m = 7$.

steps.title: Apply the parallel-lines condition
steps.content: Parallel lines have equal slopes. So line j must also have slope $m = 7$.

steps.title: Use the given point to find the y-intercept
steps.content:
Line j passes through $(0, 3)$. A point of the form $(0, b)$ is the y-intercept, so $b = 3$.
Equation of line j: $y = 7x + 3$.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = 7x + 3$': Slope = 7 (parallel to k), passes through (0, 3). ✓
  • Choice B — '$y = -3x + 3$': Slope = −3, not parallel to k (slope 7). ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = -7x + 3$': Slope = −7, not parallel to k. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 3x + 3$': Slope = 3, not parallel to k. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
